landmarkChecklist: Stay high on the right side of the tracheal column.
Use the superior right paratracheal stripe before the 4R handoff.
Bronchoscopic cue: superior right lateral tracheal wall.
Ultrasound cue: superior right paratracheal target above 4R.

ct.title: Axial CT Correlate
ct.focusLabel: Right of trachea, superior paratracheal stripe
ct.caption: Use the superior-right stack relationship first, then confirm depth above 4R.

bronchoscopy.title: Bronchoscopic Correlate
bronchoscopy.caption: Keep the scope high and right before the lower paratracheal descent.

ultrasound.title: EBUS Correlate
ultrasound.caption: Mentally stack it above 4R and separate it from the hilar pathway.

quizItems.prompt: Which station is highlighted in this high right paratracheal CT view?

landmarkChecklist: Stay high on the left side of the trachea before 4L.
Use left-sided vascular relationships to keep it separate from 4L.
Bronchoscopic cue: superior left tracheal contour, not hilar branching.
Ultrasound cue: superior left paratracheal target above the 4L window.

ct.title: Axial CT Correlate
ct.focusLabel: Left of trachea, superior paratracheal stripe
ct.caption: Read it as the upper half of the left paratracheal stack.

bronchoscopy.title: Bronchoscopic Correlate
bronchoscopy.caption: Keep the target superior and left before you move down toward 4L.

ultrasound.title: EBUS Correlate
ultrasound.caption: Confirm laterality first, then keep it superior to the 4L cue set.

quizItems.prompt: Which station is highlighted from the superior left tracheal wall perspective?

landmarkChecklist: Keep the target below 2R and proximal to the right hilar handoff.
CT cue: lower right paratracheal corridor near the main bronchus approach.
Bronchoscopic cue: right lateral tracheal wall closer to the carinal descent.
Ultrasound cue: common right paratracheal staging window beneath 2R.

ct.title: Axial CT Correlate
ct.caption: Anchor 4R beneath 2R before you transition into the hilar stations.

bronchoscopy.title: Bronchoscopic Correlate
bronchoscopy.orientation: Right lateral tracheal wall near the carinal descent.
bronchoscopy.caption: This is the right-sided anchor before the hilar split.

ultrasound.title: EBUS Correlate
ultrasound.caption: Treat 4R as the main right paratracheal target and compare it against 10R only after that.

quizItems.prompt: Which station is highlighted in this right lower paratracheal EBUS view?

landmarkChecklist: Keep the target below 2L and above the left hilar handoff.
CT cue: lower left paratracheal station along the superior-to-inferior stack.
Bronchoscopic cue: left lateral tracheal wall before hilar branching.
Ultrasound cue: left paratracheal target that learners often confuse with 10L.

ct.title: Axial CT Correlate
ct.caption: Use the left stack first, then separate 4L from the left hilar pair.

bronchoscopy.title: Bronchoscopic Correlate
bronchoscopy.orientation: Left lateral tracheal survey near the carina.
bronchoscopy.caption: Confirm that you are still paratracheal before you move into the hilar branch points.

ultrasound.title: EBUS Correlate
ultrasound.caption: The confusion pair is usually 10L, so keep the target proximal rather than hilar.

quizItems.prompt: Which station is highlighted in this lower left paratracheal CT panel?

landmarkChecklist: Use the carina as the reset point before you branch right or left.
CT cue: midline station below the carina.
Bronchoscopic cue: central landmark under the main bifurcation.
Ultrasound cue: centerline target bridging the hilar pathways.

ct.title: Axial CT Correlate
ct.caption: If you lose orientation, station 7 is the quickest midline reset.

bronchoscopy.title: Bronchoscopic Correlate
bronchoscopy.orientation: Carinal bifurcation with a target just beneath it.
bronchoscopy.caption: This is the central landmark before you commit to either hilar pathway.

ultrasound.title: EBUS Correlate
ultrasound.caption: Think below the bifurcation, not lateral to it.

quizItems.prompt: Which station is highlighted directly beneath the carinal bifurcation?

landmarkChecklist: Place it distal to 4R and proximal to 11R.
CT cue: right hilar entry zone at the main bronchus handoff.
Bronchoscopic cue: right main bronchus before more distal branching.
Ultrasound cue: hilar checkpoint, not the interlobar branch.

ct.title: Axial CT Correlate
ct.caption: The key distinction is hilar handoff rather than interlobar branching.

bronchoscopy.title: Bronchoscopic Correlate
bronchoscopy.orientation: Right main bronchus before deeper branch points.
bronchoscopy.caption: Stay at the right hilar gateway before you move to 11R.

ultrasound.title: EBUS Correlate
ultrasound.focusLabel: Right hilar target near the main bronchus entry
ultrasound.caption: If the cue feels too distal, you may have drifted into 11R.

quizItems.prompt: Which station is highlighted at the right hilar checkpoint rather than the interlobar branch?

landmarkChecklist: Place it distal to 4L and proximal to 11L.
CT cue: left hilar entry zone at the main bronchus handoff.
Bronchoscopic cue: left main bronchus before interlobar branching.
Ultrasound cue: hilar checkpoint that learners often confuse with 4L and 11L.

ct.title: Axial CT Correlate
ct.caption: Keep the target distal to the paratracheal stack but proximal to the interlobar branch.

bronchoscopy.title: Bronchoscopic Correlate
bronchoscopy.orientation: Left main bronchus before more distal branching.
bronchoscopy.caption: This is the gateway between the left paratracheal and interlobar cues.

ultrasound.title: EBUS Correlate
ultrasound.focusLabel: Left hilar target near the main bronchus entry
ultrasound.caption: Use the branch depth to separate 10L from 11L and its proximity to separate it from 4L.

quizItems.prompt: Which station is highlighted at the left hilar checkpoint on CT?

landmarkChecklist: Treat it as a downstream branch from 10R.
CT cue: right interlobar station beyond the hilar gate.
Bronchoscopic cue: branch-level right airway landmark after the main bronchus.
Ultrasound cue: more distal right hilar target compared with 10R.

ct.title: Axial CT Correlate
ct.caption: The mental shift is from gateway to branch-level anatomy.

bronchoscopy.title: Bronchoscopic Correlate
bronchoscopy.orientation: Right branch-level airway after the hilar gate.
bronchoscopy.caption: If the target feels too proximal, step back to 10R.

ultrasound.title: EBUS Correlate
ultrasound.caption: This should feel more branched and distal than the 10R view.

quizItems.prompt: Which station is highlighted after the right hilar gate at the branch level?

landmarkChecklist: Treat it as a downstream branch from 10L.
CT cue: left interlobar station beyond the hilar checkpoint.
Bronchoscopic cue: branch-level left airway landmark after the main bronchus.
Ultrasound cue: more distal left hilar target compared with 10L.

ct.title: Axial CT Correlate
ct.caption: Confirm that you are distal to the left hilar checkpoint before you call it 11L.

bronchoscopy.title: Bronchoscopic Correlate
bronchoscopy.orientation: Left branch-level airway after the hilar gate.
bronchoscopy.caption: The key distinction is branch depth rather than simple laterality.

ultrasound.title: EBUS Correlate
ultrasound.caption: This should feel more distal and branched than the 10L correlate.

quizItems.prompt: Which station is highlighted as the distal left hilar/interlobar target?
